steps.title: Distribute the coefficient into the second group
steps.content:
Distribute the \$5$ into $(x^{2}+8)$:

$$5(x^{2}+8) = 5x^{2} + 40$$

Rewrite the expression with all terms:
$$x^{3}+9x^{2}-8x + 5x^{2} + 40$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Group and combine terms by degree:
  • $x^{3}$ terms: $x^{3}$
  • $x^{2}$ terms: $9x^{2} + 5x^{2} = 14x^{2}$
  • $x$ terms: $-8x$
  • Constants: \$40$

$$x^{3}+14x^{2}-8x+40$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
